title: Allophyllum leptaleum
taxonomy: Allophyllum leptaleum is a flowering plant species in the family Polemoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Allophyllum, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the western United States. The species Allophyllum leptaleum is a shrubby perennial with small white or pink flowers that are produced in clusters. Allophyllum leptaleum is commonly known as the slender bedstraw or slender bedstraw-leptaleum, and it is found in dry areas and open woodlands. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and is often found in rocky or sandy soils.
